prompt: A 19th-century naturalist scientific illustration of a mountainous landscape shown as a cross-section terrain with multiple valleys and hills. A small sphere is shown rolling down the slopes toward the lowest valley. Rendered in fine ink lines, hatching, and muted watercolor washes on aged paper. Earth-tone palette, beige paper texture, precise scientific drawing style from an old geology or biology book. Hand-drawn, vintage natural history illustration style. No modern elements.
tradition_approach: Used ink linework, cross-hatching, muted watercolor washes, aged paper texture, and scientific cross-section composition typical of 19th-century natural history and geology illustrations.
